config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena hak individu justru membatasi kekuasaan agar tidak semena-mena. Kalau hak orang per orang terlalu mudah dikorbankan atas nama “kepentingan bersama,” itu bisa membuka jalan bagi penyalahgunaan oleh negara atau mayoritas. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan: masyarakat kuat kalau warganya dilindungi hak dasarnya, bukan saat individu selalu diminta mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights. From an American perspective, I am wary of any claim that the collective should come first by default, because that kind of thinking can easily become an excuse for government overreach or majority rule trampling minorities. At the same time, rights are not a license to ignore public safety or shared obligations, so the real answer is a balance with strong limits on when society can override an individual. If society always wins, individual liberty becomes conditional instead of fundamental, and I do not accept that.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu longgar: hak warga kecil bisa disingkirkan, sementara yang kuat lolos dari tanggung jawab. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas, karena tanpa itu mayoritas atau negara bisa bertindak semena-mena. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu meminta individu mengalah, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya secara seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in the United States we have seen too many examples of “society first” becoming a justification for trampling dissent, minorities, or unpopular individuals. That said, I also do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation; public health, safety, and basic civic duties can justify limits, but those limits need to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. The key issue is that rights should be the default, and collective goals should have to earn any override through a strong justification, not just vague appeals to the common good. If the balance tips too far toward society by default, liberty stops being a real protection and becomes a privilege the majority can revoke.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan yang paling mudah dirugikan justru warga yang lemah atau minoritas, bukan kepentingan bersama yang benar-benar adil. Hak individu harus jadi batas tegas agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena, walaupun dalam keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan publik memang bisa ada pembatasan yang ketat dan terukur. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengutamakan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan dengan hak dasar sebagai fondasi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general rule, because in the United States I see individual rights as the starting point, not something society gets to override whenever it finds it convenient. The history here is full of examples where “the public good” was used to excuse discrimination, censorship, surveillance, or other abuses against unpopular people and minorities. That does not mean society never matters; it does, but any limit on rights has to be narrow, justified, and accountable rather than assumed by default. If society always comes first, then rights are no longer rights in any meaningful sense.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
